digest.component_or_area: agent land/land-status command + outcome enum (autolander project tooling)
propositions: Provide a single agent-facing command that requests a land and returns one typed terminal outcome plus a distinct pinned exit code per outcome, collapsing monitoring to one call.

land --wait checks the bot heartbeat first and only sets the Autosubmit label under the agent's own Gerrit identity when the heartbeat is fresh; a stale heartbeat returns lander_down without setting the label.

Bot liveness is read from S5's read-only status endpoint exposing heartbeat_age_s against a 90 s staleness threshold; stale or unreachable endpoint returns lander_down immediately rather than hanging to timed_out.

Outcome derivation follows a fallback precedence where the bot's recorded outcome (S3) wins, else native Gerrit state derives ci_failed/review_failed/merged/abandoned; needs_rebase is not derivable from native state and requires the S3 record.
land-status is a single-shot variant returning the same enum plus non-terminal pending while the bot drives, and not_requested when a change has no bot record and no Autosubmit set.

The command lives in project tooling not rebar core, reuses the stdlib-only urllib+Basic-auth+XSSI Gerrit client pattern with the agent's ambient credential, and the outcome enum/exit-code table/JSON schema are documented as a versioned contract.
